channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Charles Cohen's D&D Building Default: A Pattern of Distress at 979 Third Avenue
item.description: Charles Cohen's D&D Building at 979 Third Avenue defaults on $150M CMBS loan, transferred to special servicing. Occupancy fell to 63%, revenue down 24% in a decade. Ground lease escalations compound distress.

news:news.news:title: Charles Cohen's D&D Building Default: A Pattern of Distress at 979 Third Avenue
news:news.news:keywords: Charles Cohen, 979 Third Avenue, D&D Building, CMBS default, special servicing, Green Loan Services, Cohen Brothers Realty, ground lease, Fortress Credit, foreclosure

item.title: Vornado, Stellar Bet 976 Units on Tribeca Density Play as City Scrambles for Housing
item.description: Vornado Realty Trust and Stellar Management file plans for 976-unit residential tower at Independence Plaza in Tribeca, using NYC's Universal Affordability Preference program to unlock density.

news:news.news:title: Vornado, Stellar Bet 976 Units on Tribeca Density Play as City Scrambles for Housing
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado Realty Trust, Stellar Management, Independence Plaza, Tribeca, Universal Affordability Preference, multifamily development, New York City housing

item.title: CMBS Reappraisals Hit 53% Median Discount in 2025, Urban Office Leads Losses
item.description: Trepp data shows 2025 CMBS reappraisals at 53% median discount; urban office at -64% and 2017-2018 vintage at -71% signal structural impairment, not cyclical dip.

news:news.news:title: CMBS Reappraisals Hit 53% Median Discount in 2025, Urban Office Leads Losses
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, reappraisals, office, valuation, distressed CRE

item.title: Vornado, Stellar File Plans for 72-Story Tribeca Tower as City of Yes Clears Path
item.description: Vornado and Stellar file plans for a 72-story, 976-unit tower at Independence Plaza, bypassing ULURP. The $675M CMBS refinancing and $33M in upgrades signal a new phase for the Tribeca complex.

news:news.news:title: Vornado, Stellar File Plans for 72-Story Tribeca Tower as City of Yes Clears Path
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado Realty Trust, Stellar Management, Independence Plaza, Tribeca, City of Yes, CMBS, affordable housing, ULURP

item.title: Vornado-Aurora JV Refis Chelsea Office at Wider Spreads, Longer Maturity
item.description: Vornado and Aurora Capital refinance 61 Ninth Avenue with a $161M UBS loan at SOFR+300bps, replacing a $155M loan at SOFR+245bps. The 194K SF Chelsea office building is 100% leased to Aetna and Starbucks.

news:news.news:title: Vornado-Aurora JV Refis Chelsea Office at Wider Spreads, Longer Maturity
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado Realty Trust, Aurora Capital Associates, UBS, 61 Ninth Avenue, office refinancing, Chelsea, Manhattan, interest-only loan, SOFR, Walker & Dunlop

item.title: The Day the Music Stopped
item.description: A new Fed chair, a 6% inflation bombshell, and the reckoning that American commercial real estate can no longer defer.

news:news.news:title: The Day the Music Stopped
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, inflation, PPI, commercial real estate, CRE debt, CMBS, maturity wall

item.title: Bond Yields Hit 2026 High as PPI Surge Crushes Rate-Cut Hopes
item.description: April PPI surges 1.4% monthly, 6% annual—double forecasts. 10-year Treasury hits 4.49%, 30-year above 5%. Mortgage rates set to climb further as Fed rate-cut expectations evaporate.

news:news.news:title: Bond Yields Hit 2026 High as PPI Surge Crushes Rate-Cut Hopes
news:news.news:keywords: Treasury yields, PPI, mortgage rates, Federal Reserve, inflation

item.title: Spring Market Stalls as 30-Year Fixed Hits 6.57% on Hot Inflation Data
item.description: 30-year fixed mortgage rate jumps to 6.57% after hotter PPI and CPI reports. Buyer power down 4% from February. Spring market recovery stalls as rates reverse March declines.

news:news.news:title: Spring Market Stalls as 30-Year Fixed Hits 6.57% on Hot Inflation Data
news:news.news:keywords: mortgage rates, spring market, inflation, PPI, CPI, buyer power, housing inventory

item.title: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair: CRE Braces for Rate Policy Shift
item.description: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed chair in 54-45 vote. CRE industry expects lower rates and revised bank capital rules. MBA and CREFC signal engagement.

news:news.news:title: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair: CRE Braces for Rate Policy Shift
news:news.news:keywords: Kevin Warsh, Federal Reserve, interest rates, CRE capital requirements, monetary policy

item.title: Brookfield and Qatar Tap $1.9B Refi for Two Manhattan West, Pocket $273M
item.description: Brookfield and QIA secure $1.9B refinancing for Two Manhattan West via Wells Fargo, extracting $273M in cash. The 97%-leased tower at $132/sf gross rent signals a trophy office refinancing wave.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield and Qatar Tap $1.9B Refi for Two Manhattan West, Pocket $273M
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, Qatar Investment Authority, Two Manhattan West, refinancing, Wells Fargo

item.title: Brookfield, QIA Secure $1.9B CMBS Loan at 107 bps Over Treasuries as Office Market Fractures
item.description: Brookfield and QIA refinance 2 Manhattan West with a $1.9B CMBS loan at 107 bps over the 10-Year Treasury. The 5.53% coupon and $273M cash return signal a bifurcated office market where only the strongest assets access public debt.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield, QIA Secure $1.9B CMBS Loan at 107 bps Over Treasuries as Office Market Fractures
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, Brookfield, Qatar Investment Authority, Manhattan office, refinancing, Wells Fargo

item.title: Office Loans Drive CMBS Special Servicing Rate to 11.38% in April
item.description: CMBS special servicing rate rose 38 bps to 11.38% in April, driven by office loans. $1.93B in new transfers. Office rate up 93 bps. Multifamily up 33 bps.

news:news.news:title: Office Loans Drive CMBS Special Servicing Rate to 11.38% in April
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, special servicing, office, delinquency, Trepp

item.title: Bond Bears Reload: Rate Hike Bets Return as Inflation Sticks
item.description: Traders renew bearish Treasury bets as oil and inflation push higher, lifting expectations for Fed rate hikes. The shift threatens CRE debt costs and cap rates.

news:news.news:title: Bond Bears Reload: Rate Hike Bets Return as Inflation Sticks
news:news.news:keywords: Treasuries, Federal Reserve, inflation, interest rates, CRE capital markets

item.title: Inflation Hits 3.8%, Mortgage Rate Relief Off the Table as Fed Hike Risk Emerges
item.description: April CPI jumps to 3.8%, highest in three years. 10-year Treasury yields rise. Mortgage rates could hit 7%. Fed rate cuts off the table; hike scenario introduced.

news:news.news:title: Inflation Hits 3.8%, Mortgage Rate Relief Off the Table as Fed Hike Risk Emerges
news:news.news:keywords: inflation, mortgage rates, Federal Reserve, CPI, 10-year Treasury, housing market

item.title: Albany’s $8B Bailout Lets Mamdani Avoid Property Tax Hike, Targets Second Homes
item.description: NYC Mayor Mamdani’s FY2027 budget avoids property tax hike thanks to $8B state aid. Pied-à-terre tax targets second homes over $5M, raising $500M annually. Capital markets watch for impact on luxury real estate.

news:news.news:title: Albany’s $8B Bailout Lets Mamdani Avoid Property Tax Hike, Targets Second Homes
news:news.news:keywords: New York City budget, pied-à-terre tax, Zohran Mamdani, Kathy Hochul, municipal finance

item.title: April CPI Surge to 3.8% Locks Fed Hold, Raises Rate-Hike Odds to 30%
item.description: April CPI hits 3.8% annual, highest since May 2023. Energy costs surge 17.9% YoY. Fed rate-hike probability rises to 30%. 10-year Treasury yields climb to 4.44%.

news:news.news:title: April CPI Surge to 3.8% Locks Fed Hold, Raises Rate-Hike Odds to 30%
news:news.news:keywords: CPI, Federal Reserve, inflation, interest rates, Treasury yields

item.title: Digital Realty Targets $3B Cornerstone Raise for US Open-End Fund
item.description: Digital Realty plans a US open-end fund with a cornerstone raise of up to $3B, signaling institutional appetite for data center exposure via private vehicles.

news:news.news:title: Digital Realty Targets $3B Cornerstone Raise for US Open-End Fund
news:news.news:keywords: Digital Realty, data centers, open-end fund, private capital, cornerstone raise

item.title: Bert Dweck Faces $14.4M in Lawsuits as Escrow Allegations Mount
item.description: Bert Dweck faces seven lawsuits seeking $14.4M over unpaid loans, bounced checks, and missing escrow funds, revealing cracks in Brooklyn's tight-knit real estate community.

news:news.news:title: Bert Dweck Faces $14.4M in Lawsuits as Escrow Allegations Mount
news:news.news:keywords: Bert Dweck, Dweck Group, lawsuits, escrow, Brooklyn real estate

item.title: Manhattan Luxury Sales Surge 80% as Pied-à-Terre Tax Looms
item.description: Manhattan luxury contracts above $10M surged 80% in April-May despite Mayor Mamdani's proposed pied-à-terre tax. Olshan Realty data shows $1.12B in volume. Market tests political risk.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Luxury Sales Surge 80% as Pied-à-Terre Tax Looms
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan luxury real estate, pied-à-terre tax, Zohran Mamdani, Ken Griffin, Olshan Realty, high-end sales

item.title: 2 World Trade Center Rises From 14-Year Pause as Amex Commits to 1.95M SF
item.description: Silverstein Properties restarts 2 World Trade Center after 14-year pause. American Express anchors 1.95M SF tower. Completion set for 2031. $5.9B economic impact projected.

news:news.news:title: 2 World Trade Center Rises From 14-Year Pause as Amex Commits to 1.95M SF
news:news.news:keywords: Silverstein Properties, 2 World Trade Center, American Express, office construction, Manhattan Financial District, Foster + Partners, supertall skyscraper

item.title: RXR Markets 325,000 RSF at 1285 Sixth Avenue: A Bet on Flight-to-Quality
item.description: RXR lists 325,000 RSF of contiguous office at 1285 Avenue of the Americas. The block spans floors 3–7, with floor plates up to 75,865 RSF. A test of flight-to-quality demand in Midtown.

news:news.news:title: RXR Markets 325,000 RSF at 1285 Sixth Avenue: A Bet on Flight-to-Quality
news:news.news:keywords: RXR, 1285 Avenue of the Americas, Midtown office, flight-to-quality, contiguous space

item.title: TPG, GFP Score $288M for 222 Broadway Office-to-Resi Conversion at 70% Basis Discount
item.description: TPG Real Estate and GFP Real Estate secure $288M construction financing from BDT & MSD for 222 Broadway office-to-residential conversion, purchased at $147.5M—70% below 2014 peak.

news:news.news:title: TPG, GFP Score $288M for 222 Broadway Office-to-Resi Conversion at 70% Basis Discount
news:news.news:keywords: office-to-residential conversion, 222 Broadway, TPG Real Estate, GFP Real Estate, BDT & MSD, CetraRuddy, Newmark, construction financing, Lower Manhattan, adaptive reuse

item.title: AI Tenants Push Manhattan Office Leasing Past Apocalypse Narrative
item.description: Anthropic nears 365K sf lease at 330 Hudson; Norm AI takes top floors at 1 WTC. AI tenants are absorbing Manhattan office space, pushing towers to 97% leased and challenging the AI-apocalypse thesis.

news:news.news:title: AI Tenants Push Manhattan Office Leasing Past Apocalypse Narrative
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan office, AI leasing, Anthropic, Norm AI, Harvey AI, SL Green, Durst, Port Authority, office market

item.title: Griffin's 350 Park Ave. Commitment Hinges on July Deadline as Mayor's Rhetoric Escalates
item.description: Vornado's 350 Park Ave. project faces a July 2026 exit deadline if Ken Griffin's Citadel pulls out after Mayor Mamdani's attack. CBRE's Siegel calls the mayor 'dangerous.'

news:news.news:title: Griffin's 350 Park Ave. Commitment Hinges on July Deadline as Mayor's Rhetoric Escalates
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado, Ken Griffin, Citadel, 350 Park Avenue, Zohran Mamdani, office development, New York City real estate

item.title: NYSCRF Pours $900M Into Real Estate Funds in Single Month
item.description: NYSCRF commits $900M to real estate in March: $300M to GID Mainstay (B-class multifamily), $200M to Asana Partners IV (value-add retail), $400M to Blue Owl funds. Institutional capital shifts toward yield and operational intensity.

news:news.news:title: NYSCRF Pours $900M Into Real Estate Funds in Single Month
news:news.news:keywords: NYSCRF, pension fund, real estate allocations, GID, Asana Partners, Blue Owl Capital, multifamily, value-add retail, institutional capital

item.title: Cord Meyer Secures $150M New York Life Loan for Queens Retail as Grocery-Anchored Assets Draw Institutional Capital
item.description: JLL arranges $150M New York Life Real Estate Investors loan for Cord Meyer's Bay Terrace Shopping Center in Queens. Grocery-anchored asset at 90% leased as retail construction hits 15K SF vs 150K SF average.

news:news.news:title: Cord Meyer Secures $150M New York Life Loan for Queens Retail as Grocery-Anchored Assets Draw Institutional Capital
news:news.news:keywords: Cord Meyer, JLL Capital Markets, New York Life Real Estate Investors, Bay Terrace Shopping Center, Queens retail

item.title: Madison Realty Capital Delivers 16 Fifth Avenue: A Bet on Greenwich Village Luxury Condos
item.description: Madison Realty Capital and City Urban Realty complete 16 Fifth Avenue, a 19-story, 14-unit condominium in Greenwich Village. The $27.5M land buy in 2015 now yields a Robert A.M. Stern-designed tower. Sales launch via Corcoran Sunshine.

news:news.news:title: Madison Realty Capital Delivers 16 Fifth Avenue: A Bet on Greenwich Village Luxury Condos
news:news.news:keywords: Madison Realty Capital, 16 Fifth Avenue, Robert A.M. Stern Architects, Greenwich Village, luxury condos

item.title: Madison Realty Cuts Penthouse Price 36% as Greenwich Village Sellout Lags
item.description: Madison Realty Capital sells 16 Fifth Ave penthouse for $32.5M, 36% off its $45M ask. Only 5 of 14 units closed; projected $288M sellout faces headwinds as construction disputes and buyer caution persist.

news:news.news:title: Madison Realty Cuts Penthouse Price 36% as Greenwich Village Sellout Lags
news:news.news:keywords: Madison Realty Capital, 16 Fifth Avenue, Greenwich Village, penthouse discount, luxury condo market

item.title: Alchemy Pays $57M for Tribeca Garage, Betting on Ultra-Luxury Condo Demand
item.description: Alchemy Properties buys vacant Tribeca parking garage for $57M, plans 11 ultra-luxury condo units. Deal closed at discount from $75M ask, signaling developer confidence in Manhattan's luxury market.

news:news.news:title: Alchemy Pays $57M for Tribeca Garage, Betting on Ultra-Luxury Condo Demand
news:news.news:keywords: Alchemy Properties, Tribeca, condo development, parking garage, Manhattan investment sales

item.title: Rogers Equities Pays $30M for Brooklyn Heights Walgreens, Plans 75-Unit Tower
item.description: Rogers Equities buys 120 Court Street for $30M from Sorkin family. Walgreens lease expires August 2026. Plans filed for 75-unit, 14-story rental under 485x program.

news:news.news:title: Rogers Equities Pays $30M for Brooklyn Heights Walgreens, Plans 75-Unit Tower
news:news.news:keywords: Rogers Equities, Brooklyn Heights, Walgreens, 485x, development site, Newmark

item.title: CMBS Issuance Hits $32.7B in Q1 2026 as Distress Rises to 7.55%
item.description: CMBS issuance hits $32.7B in Q1 2026, second-busiest since pre-GFC, as distress rises to 7.55% and $76.6B in hard maturities loom. Office leads delinquencies.

news:news.news:title: CMBS Issuance Hits $32.7B in Q1 2026 as Distress Rises to 7.55%
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, CRE lending, distress, office, maturities

item.title: Manhattan Tech Leasing Shatters Record in 2025, AI Drives 2026 Surge
item.description: Manhattan tech tenants signed 235 leases in 2025, a record. AI accounted for 33% of Q1 2026 demand. Concessions hit 13.3 months free rent. Midtown South captured two-thirds of activity.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Tech Leasing Shatters Record in 2025, AI Drives 2026 Surge
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan office, tech leasing, AI real estate, Colliers report, concessions

item.title: Steve Roth’s Penn District Bet Hangs on a Pied-à-Terre Tax Spat
item.description: Steve Roth blasts Mayor Mamdani over Ken Griffin pied-à-terre tax video. Vornado faces July deadline on 350 Park Avenue JV as political friction threatens $1.5M sf office tower.

news:news.news:title: Steve Roth’s Penn District Bet Hangs on a Pied-à-Terre Tax Spat
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado, Steve Roth, Ken Griffin, Zohran Mamdani, pied-à-terre tax, 350 Park Avenue, office development, New York City politics

item.title: Brookfield Lands 20-Year, 475K SF Renewal at One Liberty as Law Firm Doubles Down on Lower Manhattan
item.description: Cleary Gottlieb renews 475K SF at Brookfield's One Liberty for 20 years. The law firm takes top 10 floors in a 2.3M SF complex recently renovated. Newmark and Cushman & Wakefield advised.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield Lands 20-Year, 475K SF Renewal at One Liberty as Law Firm Doubles Down on Lower Manhattan
news:news.news:keywords: Cleary Gottlieb, One Liberty, Brookfield Properties, office lease renewal, Lower Manhattan

item.title: Vornado Declares Manhattan Office Recovery—But Warns Politics Could Undermine It
item.description: Vornado's Steve Roth declares Manhattan office recovery, citing 12M sf leasing Q1 and replacement costs above $200 psf. Warns pied-à-terre tax could deter capital like Ken Griffin's.

news:news.news:title: Vornado Declares Manhattan Office Recovery—But Warns Politics Could Undermine It
news:news.news:keywords: Vornado, Manhattan office, Steve Roth, Ken Griffin, pied-à-terre tax, 350 Park Avenue, office recovery, replacement cost

item.title: Garment District Rezoning: 1% of Housing Goal, 100% of Developer Headaches
item.description: Marty Burger's 107-unit project at 29 West 35th Street is the only new development from NYC's Midtown South rezoning. 485x prevailing wage rules and construction costs stall pipeline.

news:news.news:title: Garment District Rezoning: 1% of Housing Goal, 100% of Developer Headaches
news:news.news:keywords: Midtown South rezoning, 485x, Marty Burger, Garment District, New York City housing

item.title: Cleary Gottlieb's 475K SF Lease: Manhattan's Largest Is Actually a Downsizing
item.description: Cleary Gottlieb's 475K SF lease at One Liberty Plaza is a 75K SF downsizing from its 2007 deal. The largest April lease in Manhattan signals tenant contraction, not expansion.

news:news.news:title: Cleary Gottlieb's 475K SF Lease: Manhattan's Largest Is Actually a Downsizing
news:news.news:keywords: Cleary Gottlieb, One Liberty Plaza, Manhattan office leasing, Brookfield Properties, downsizing, law firm real estate

item.title: FHA Rule Change Pushes 250,000 Borrowers Toward Default, Forced Sale
item.description: New FHA loss mitigation rules cap partial claims at one per two years, requiring three consecutive payments. Up to 250,000 borrowers face foreclosure or short sale, with price impacts varying by market strength.

news:news.news:title: FHA Rule Change Pushes 250,000 Borrowers Toward Default, Forced Sale
news:news.news:keywords: FHA, loss mitigation, foreclosure, distressed sales, housing policy

item.title: Brooklyn Appellate Court Revives Karp’s Loan-to-Own Claim Against Madison Realty Capital
item.description: Brooklyn appellate court revives Eli Karp’s lawsuit alleging Madison Realty Capital orchestrated a loan-to-own scheme on the Hello Nostrand project, seeking damages for fraud and breach of contract.

news:news.news:title: Brooklyn Appellate Court Revives Karp’s Loan-to-Own Claim Against Madison Realty Capital
news:news.news:keywords: Eli Karp, Madison Realty Capital, loan-to-own, Hello Nostrand, Brooklyn appellate court

item.title: Blackstone's $1.7B Data Center REIT IPO: AI Demand Meets Permanent Capital
item.description: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust files $1.7B IPO to acquire data centers leased to investment-grade tenants. AI demand, power constraints, and REIT structure target permanent capital for hyperscale assets.

news:news.news:title: Blackstone's $1.7B Data Center REIT IPO: AI Demand Meets Permanent Capital
news:news.news:keywords: Blackstone, data center, REIT IPO, AI infrastructure, capital markets

item.title: Castle Peak's Weinberg: Vertical Integration Is the Only Hedge Against Hospitality's Margin Squeeze
item.description: Castle Peak's Ben Weinberg argues vertical integration and operator mindset are critical as hospitality faces a pricing reset. Asset management replaces financial engineering in a higher-rate world.

news:news.news:title: Castle Peak's Weinberg: Vertical Integration Is the Only Hedge Against Hospitality's Margin Squeeze
news:news.news:keywords: hospitality, asset management, vertical integration, Castle Peak, Ben Weinberg, pricing reset

item.title: FHA Loss Mitigation Cuts Threaten 250,000 Borrowers With Forced Sales
item.description: New FHA rules limit loss mitigation to one partial claim every two years, pushing 250,000 delinquent borrowers toward foreclosure or short sale. Regional price impacts vary.

news:news.news:title: FHA Loss Mitigation Cuts Threaten 250,000 Borrowers With Forced Sales
news:news.news:keywords: FHA, loss mitigation, foreclosure, distressed sales, housing policy

item.title: Hines’ Luthman: M&A Surge Signals End of Financial Engineering Era
item.description: Hines' Steve Luthman says 40% M&A surge reflects structural shift to operational value creation. Operator-led platforms, not financial engineering, now drive returns.

news:news.news:title: Hines’ Luthman: M&A Surge Signals End of Financial Engineering Era
news:news.news:keywords: Hines, Steve Luthman, M&A, operational value, real estate consolidation

item.title: Global Holdings, MAG Partners Plan 149-Unit Tower on Trinity Church Ground Lease in Hudson Square
item.description: Global Holdings and MAG Partners plan a 149-unit residential tower at 122 Varick Street on a Trinity Church ground lease. 25% affordable under 485-x. 192,000 sf.

news:news.news:title: Global Holdings, MAG Partners Plan 149-Unit Tower on Trinity Church Ground Lease in Hudson Square
news:news.news:keywords: Global Holdings, MAG Partners, Trinity Church, Hudson Square, 485-x

item.title: Bond Market Braces for Treasury Refunding, Fed Speakers, Jobs Data
item.description: US bond traders focus on Treasury's quarterly refunding, Fed speakers, and May jobs data. Implications for CRE debt costs and capital flows.

news:news.news:title: Bond Market Braces for Treasury Refunding, Fed Speakers, Jobs Data
news:news.news:keywords: Treasury refunding, Federal Reserve, jobs report, bond yields, CRE capital markets

item.title: Chetrit's $132 Million Reckoning: How a Four-Decade Empire Cracks
item.description: Maverick Real Estate pursues $132M judgment against Meyer Chetrit via asset auction. Defaults, fraud allegations, and a $39M fashion-fire ruling pile up.

news:news.news:title: Chetrit's $132 Million Reckoning: How a Four-Decade Empire Cracks
news:news.news:keywords: Chetrit Group, Maverick Real Estate Partners, NYC Commercial Real Estate, Distressed Assets, CRE Defaults

item.title: NYC Lenders Deployed $2.1B in Five March Deals as Office Bets Return
item.description: Five NYC CRE loans totaling $2.13B closed in March 2026, led by M&T Bank's $460M Domino Sugar construction loan and Wells Fargo's $450M CMBS deal at 1250 Broadway.

news:news.news:title: NYC Lenders Deployed $2.1B in Five March Deals as Office Bets Return
news:news.news:keywords: Construction Lending, CMBS, NYC Office Market, Multifamily Development, Debt Markets

item.title: Senate's Seven-Year Sell-Off Clause Halts BTR Lending Nationwide
item.description: A Senate bill mandating institutional investors sell 350+ homes within 7 years has frozen BTR financing nationwide, stalling $500M+ in Phoenix alone.

news:news.news:title: Senate's Seven-Year Sell-Off Clause Halts BTR Lending Nationwide
news:news.news:keywords: Build-to-Rent, BTR Financing, Federal Housing Policy, Freddie Mac, Phoenix CRE

item.title: Sovereign Partners Pays $378M for 575 Fifth Avenue in Grand Central Bet
item.description: Sovereign Partners acquired 575 Fifth Avenue from Beacon Capital for $316M, plus $62M in units from MetLife—totaling $378M for 371,374 sq ft in Grand Central.

news:news.news:title: Sovereign Partners Pays $378M for 575 Fifth Avenue in Grand Central Bet
news:news.news:keywords: Office Investment, Grand Central, Beacon Capital Partners, Sovereign Partners, NYC Capital Markets

item.title: NYC Pulls Back From $2B Hudson Yards Rail Deck Deal With Related
item.description: NYC Mayor Mamdani distances from Adams' $2B Hudson Yards platform deal with Related Cos., threatening 4,000 units and a city-backed debt structure.

news:news.news:title: NYC Pulls Back From $2B Hudson Yards Rail Deck Deal With Related
news:news.news:keywords: Hudson Yards, Related Companies, NYC Policy, Tax Exemption, Construction Financing
